claim_gate.reason: Explicit overlapping signed-product U-statistics do not provide a joint-polynomial decoder. An implicit contraction or a different estimator/measurement class remains a substantive open route.

estimator_classes.remaining_obligation: Change the kernel or estimator class; overlap alone does not remove the variance bound.
estimator_classes.theorem_scope: symmetric multilinear product kernel evaluated on every r-subset

estimator_classes.remaining_obligation: Give an implicit contraction rather than enumerating the certified number of tuples.
estimator_classes.theorem_scope: degree may grow and records may become polynomial, but required evaluated tuple count remains exponential for coarse buckets

estimator_classes.remaining_obligation: Prove polynomial contraction cost, uniform margin, precision, and absence of an N-sized hidden spectrum.
estimator_classes.theorem_scope: variance bound applies, but arithmetic cost need not equal tuple count if the kernel factorizes

estimator_classes.remaining_obligation: State the observation polynomial, decision tree, shared-sample error, and worst-reflection cost exactly.
estimator_classes.theorem_scope: outside the single symmetric signed-product kernel model

estimator_classes.remaining_obligation: Supply a polynomial circuit and exact f=1/lattice composition theorem.

falsifiers_triggered: Overlap and Hoeffding degeneracy cannot reduce U-statistic variance below kernel variance divided by C(m,r).
For coarse polynomially many buckets, the required tuple count is exponential for every tested degree.
Fixed degree retains exponential record complexity even when all overlapping tuples are used.
Growing degree can make the record count polynomial only while explicit tuple evaluation remains exponential.
The theorem does not rule out a polynomial implicit contraction, a non-product adaptive statistic, or a collective measurement.

model.accuracy: complex MSE at most gamma^2/4 for every hidden reflection
model.statistic: U_m is the average of h over all C(m,r) distinct r-subsets
model.target: uniform expected-score margin for one frequency bucket versus its complement

summary: Certified 140 overlapping U-statistic rows across 7 degrees. Joint polynomial explicit rows=0; implicit-contraction lower bounds=0.

theorem.coefficient_minimum: C(r,s)/C(m,s) is minimized at s=r, hence Var(U_m)>=Var(h)/C(m,r)
theorem.fixed_degree_consequence: m=2^Omega(n/r) for B=poly(n) and constant r
theorem.growing_degree_consequence: explicit evaluation still uses 2^Omega(n) tuple terms for B=poly(n)
theorem.scope: single symmetric signed-product U-statistic with explicit uniform margin/MSE contract
